description: Trigger-accuracy and behavioural evals. 6 trigger evals check whether the skill should fire. 9 behavioural evals exercise the 10 SKILL.md acceptance tests (Tests 1-10); the vague-input case (Test 4) is covered at the trigger layer by trigger-006, which fires the skill and expects an INSUFFICIENT_INPUT verdict.

evals.prompt: We're starting the buyer-tagging sync project. Define the destination for it — what does done actually look like?
expectations: Skill runs the MVI gate before authoring anything
Skill runs Element 0 (the three-way scope gate) first
Skill walks Elements 1-5 and writes DESTINATION.md at the project root
DESTINATION.md frontmatter carries the generative_primitive tag with generated_at and an absolute-path project_root

evals.prompt: Before we do any more work on this venture I want a written statement of where we're headed and how we'd know we got there.
expectations: Skill authors a destination with a third-party-observable binary success test (Element 2)
Skill produces a could-the-test-lie clause (Element 4) distinct from the still-true-later clause (Element 3)
Output is written to DESTINATION.md, not returned only inline

evals.prompt: Author a destination for this workshop — it's an open-ended R&D thinking space with no fixed end point.
expectations: Skill runs Element 0 and the operator can answer 'No, and not ever'
On no-forever: skill REFUSES to write a hollow destination, verdict == SCOPE_REFUSAL
Skill routes to /reduce-to-first-principles and /map-feedback-loops; no DESTINATION.md written

evals.prompt: Show me today's destination glance before the task list.
expectations: Skill is not invoked; the daily destination glance is the daily-plan flow's job, not this skill's
If invoked anyway: skill clarifies it authors/reviews destinations, it does not run the daily glance

evals.prompt: Is the question 'adopt Hermes or build native' framed right? Audit it.
expectations: Skill is not invoked; auditing an existing framing is /reduce-to-first-principles' job
This skill AUTHORS a destination; it does not diagnose an existing question

evals.prompt: Define the destination for making the business better.
expectations: Skill IS invoked — the explicit trigger phrase 'Define the destination for' routes here; the skill is what runs the MVI gate
MVI gate fails — 'make the business better' names no observable change
verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT with an example_input template; no destination fabricated

evals.prompt: Define the destination for the buyer-tagging sync: it pulls tagged buyers from the CRM into the deal-matching pipeline so dispo can match a new deal to ready buyers fast.
expectations: Scope gate answered Yes; verdict == PASS; scope_gate_branch == yes
DESTINATION.md written with status: confirmed, version: 1, written: true, reviewed_advanced: true
Element 1 is a condition in the world, not an output ('X was shipped')
Element 2 is a third-party-observable binary test
Frontmatter carries provenance: generative_primitive, generated_at, an absolute-path project_root, reviewed

evals.prompt: Define the destination. The end-state is: the buyer-tagging feature is shipped to production.
expectations: The output-vs-condition general test fires (an artefact + a verb of creation)
Skill restates 'shipped' as the condition the output was meant to create and asks the operator to confirm
guards_fired includes the output-as-end-state restatement; recipe.element_1_end_state is a condition

evals.prompt: Define the destination. The success test is: the founding team agrees we should keep going.
expectations: The third-party-observable general test fires — internal agreement is not externally checkable
Skill asks for the external evidence the agreement would rest on and uses that as the test
guards_fired includes the internal-consensus challenge; element_2_binary_test is third-party-observable

evals.prompt: Define the destination for this project. (A DESTINATION.md already exists at the project root.)
expectations: Skill detects review mode; surfaces the current destination; does NOT overwrite silently
Choice (a) still-holds: verdict == PASS, written == false, reviewed_advanced == true, no archive
Choice (b) without confirm_overwrite: verdict == OVERWRITE_BLOCKED, no write
Choice (b) with confirm_overwrite: prior file archived as DESTINATION.v1.md, new version: 2
Choice (c) abandon: verdict == PASS, written == false, reviewed_advanced == false, file unchanged

evals.prompt: Define the destination for our research track — it's pure open-ended exploration; there's no measurable end-state and there never will be.
expectations: Element 0 scope gate answered 'No, and not ever'; verdict == SCOPE_REFUSAL
scope_gate_branch == no_forever; destination_file.written == false — no hollow destination authored
hand_offs names /reduce-to-first-principles and /map-feedback-loops; the refusal records the gate was run

evals.prompt: Define the destination for this venture — we have a direction but the success metric isn't defined yet.
expectations: Element 0 scope gate answered 'Not yet'; the skill asks the one lightweight question
verdict == DRAFT; scope_gate_branch == not_yet; DESTINATION.md status: DRAFT — UNCONFIRMED
Elements 1-2 carry the literal HYPOTHESIS: prefix; Elements 3-5 are also populated on top of them
No project_scope supplied; MVI gate fails; verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT
mvi.missing includes project_scope; a structured example_input block is returned; no destination fabricated

evals.prompt: Define the destination for the depot's new third delivery route — a freight-operations project.
expectations: A valid six-part artefact is produced with generic vocabulary — no workshop/synthesis-programme assumptions
The recipe applies cleanly in the freight domain; the elements are freight-relevant

evals.prompt: Take the DESTINATION.md this project authored and feed its end-state to /reduce-to-first-principles to audit the destination question.
expectations: This is the Component 1 end-to-end integration check — define-destination is NOT re-invoked here; /reduce-to-first-principles is
/reduce-to-first-principles receives hypothesis_provenance: generative_primitive with a source_hypothesis built from the DESTINATION.md frontmatter (generated_at + absolute-path project_root)
Branch D fires; the bounds check passes (same project, in-window); the destination is ACCEPTED — anti_anchoring.verdict in {AGREED, DISAGREED, INCONCLUSIVE}, never validate_upstream_*, never blocked
